# Selected works — close-out comment for battle test issue

Post this as a comment on the issue after creation.

---

## Selected works

| 5 | Poems, Household Edition | 12843 | Poetry collection; tests verse TOC and poem-level headings |

| 7 | The Conduct of Life | 39827 | Essay collection with titled chapters |
| 8 | English Traits | 39862 | Travel/cultural essays; chapter-based structure |
| 9 | Society and Solitude: Twelve Chapters | 69258 | Numbered essay chapters; tests explicit chapter count structure |
| 10 | Miscellanies | 71683 | Collected addresses, lectures, and essays; mixed structural types |
| 11 | Letters and Social Aims | 71393 | Late essay collection |
| 12 | Lectures and Biographical Sketches | 75942 | Mixed lectures and biographical essays; tests heterogeneous section types |
| 13 | Natural History of Intellect | 71558 | Late posthumous essays and papers |

| 16 | Correspondence of Carlyle and Emerson, Vol. II | 13660 | Epistolary; companion volume |
| 17 | Compensation | 73035 | Single essay reprint; tests minimal/flat structure |

## Deduplication

100 of 101 works confirmed absent from:
- GitHub Issues with `test` label on `textualist/gutenbit` (#162, #164, #166, #168)
- `tests/test_battle.py` (86 distinct PG IDs)
- `.claude/skills/gutenbit-live-battle-test/references/kei-17-corpus.md` (28 entries)
- Combined exclusion set: 304+ unique PG IDs from issues + 86 from tests + corpus (with overlaps)

One work is deliberately re-included despite existing coverage:
- ⭐ PG 15 — Moby-Dick (Melville) — in test_battle.py and kei-17 corpus, but included as a
  high-value canonical work that must parse perfectly; re-test end-to-end

Two works by these authors were in the exclusion set and are not included:
- PG 42 — Jekyll and Hyde (Stevenson) — in test_battle.py
- PG 120 — Treasure Island (Stevenson) — in test_battle.py
